title: The Three Characteristics..To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash plugin
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and systematic coverage of all three characteristics (anicca, dukkha, anatta), explaining their interrelationships and practical applications in meditation. It offers rich teaching on how impermanence leads to both suffering and not-self insights, includes memorable examples (the schizophrenic monk, the corpse meditation story), and flows naturally from foundational concepts through to advanced practice guidance. The structure moves logically from the Four Noble Truths through each characteristic with clear explanations of their practical investigation.
summary: A comprehensive dharma talk exploring the three characteristics of existence—impermanence, suffering, and not-self—as interconnected aspects of Buddhist practice. Bhante explains how investigating impermanence serves as a gateway to understanding both suffering and not-self, offering practical meditation guidance and vivid examples throughout.

relevance.note: Directly about anicca, dukkha, anatta - the three characteristics
